author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans

ccel.title: NPNF1-11. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Acts of the Apostles and the Epistle to the Romans

title: Homily XXV on Rom. xiv. 1, 2.

source.title: NPNF1-11. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Acts of the Apostles and the Epistle to the Romans

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Him that is weak in the faith receive ye, but not to doubtful disputations.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For one believeth that he may eat all things; another, who is weak, eateth herbs.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: I Am aware that to most what is here said is a difficulty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And therefore I must first give the subject of the whole of this passage, and what he wishes to correct in writing this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What does he wish to correct then?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: There were many of the Jews which believed, who adhered of conscience to the Law, and after their believing, still kept to the observance of meats, as not having courage yet to quit the service of the Law entirely.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then that they might not be observed if they kept from swine’s flesh only, they abstained in consequence from all flesh, and ate herbs only, that what they were doing might have more the appearance of a fast than of observance of the Law.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Others again were farther advanced, (τελειότεροι) and kept up no one thing of the kind, who became to those, who did keep them, distressing and offensive, by reproaching them, accusing them, driving them to despondency.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Therefore the blessed Paul, out of fear lest, from a wish to be right about a trifle, they should overthrow the whole, and from a wish to bring them to indifferency about what they ate, should put them in a fair way for deserting the faith, and out of a zeal to put everything right at once, before the fit opportunity was come, should do mischief on vital points, so by this continual rebuking setting them adrift from their agreement in (ὁμολογίας εἰς) Christ, and so they should remain not righted in either respect: observe what great judgment he uses and how he concerns himself with both interests with his customary wisdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For neither does he venture to say to those who rebuke, Ye are doing amiss, that he may not seem to be confirming the other in their observances; nor again, Ye are doing right, lest he should make them the more vehement accusers: but he makes his rebuke to square with each.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And in appearance he is rebuking the stronger, but he pours forth all he has to say against the other in his address to these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the kind of correction most likely to be less grating is, when a person addresses some one else, while he is striking a blow at a different person, since this does not permit the person rebuked to fly into a passion, and introduces the medicine of correction unperceived.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: See now with what judgment he does this, and how well-timed he is with it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For after saying, “make not provision for the flesh to fulfil the lusts thereof,” then he proceeds to the discussion of these points, that he might not seem to be speaking in defence of those who were the rebukers, and were for eating of anything.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the weaker part ever requires more forethought.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Wherefore he aims his blow against the strong, immediately saying as follows, “Him that is weak in the faith.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: You see one blow immediately given to him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For by calling him weak (ἀσθενοὕντα), he points out that he is not healthy (ἄρρωστον).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then he adds next, “receive,” and point out again that he requires much attention.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this is a sign of extreme debility.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Not to doubtful disputations.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: See, he has laid on a third stripe.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For here he makes it appear that his error is of such a nature, that even those who do not transgress in the same manner, and who nevertheless admit him to their affection, and are earnestly bent upon curing him, are at doubt.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: You see how in appearance he is conversing with these, but is rebuking others secretly and without giving offence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then by placing them beside each other, one he gives encomiums, the other accusations.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he goes on to say, “One believeth that he may eat all things,” commending him on the score of his faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Another who is weak, eateth herbs,” disparaging this one again, on the score of his weakness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then since the blow he had given was deadly (καιρίαν, used hyperbolically), he comforts him again in these words,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Let not him that eateth, despise him that eateth not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He does not say, let him alone, nor does he say, do not blame him, nor yet, do not set him right; but do not reproach him, do not “despise” him, to show they were doing a thing perfectly ridiculous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But of this he speaks in other words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Let not him which eateth not, judge him that eateth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For as the more advanced made light of these, as of little faith, and falsely healed, and spurious, and still Judaizers, so they too judged these as law-breakers, or as given to gluttony.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And of these it is likely that many were of the Gentiles too.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Wherefore he proceeds, for God hath received him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But in the other’s case he does not say this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And yet to be despised was the eater’s share, as a glutton, but to be judged, his that did not eat, as of little faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But he has made them change places, to show that he not only does not deserve to be despised, but that he can even despise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But do I condemn him? he means.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: By no means.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For this is why he proceeds, “for God hath received him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Why then speakest thou to him of the law, as to a transgressor?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For God hath received him:” that is, has shown His unspeakable grace about him, and hath freed him from all charges against him; then again he turns to the strong.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Who art thou that judgest another man’s servant?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Whence it appears that they too judged, and did not despise only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “To his own Master he standeth or falleth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: See here is another stroke.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And the indignation seems to be against the strong man, and he attacks him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: When he says, “Yea, he shall be holden up,” he shows that he is still wavering, and requireth so much attention as to call in God as a physician for this, “for God,” he says, “is able to make him stand.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this we say of things we are quite in despair about.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then, that he may not despair he both gives him the name of a servant when he says, “Who art thou that judgest another man’s servant?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And here again he secretly attacks him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it is not because he does things worthy to exempt him from being judged, that I bid you not judge him, but because he is Another’s servant, that is, not thine, but God’s.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then to solace him again he does not say, “falleth,” but what? “standeth or falleth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But whether it be the latter or the former, either of these is the Master’s concernment, since the loss also goes to Him, if he does fall, as the riches too, if he stand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this again if we do not attend to Paul’s aim in not wishing them to be rebuked before a fitting opportunity, is very unworthy of the mutual care becoming for Christians.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But (as I am always saying) we must examine the mind with which it is spoken, and the subject on which it is said and the object he would compass when he says it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But he makes them respectful by no slight motive, when he says this: for what he means is, if God, Who undergoeth the loss, hitherto doth nothing, how can you be else than ill-timed and out of all measure exact, when you seize on (ἄγκων, throttle) him and annoy him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “One man esteemeth one day above another, another esteemeth every day alike.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Here he seems to me to be giving a gentle hint about fasting.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it is not unlikely that some who fasted were always judging those who did not, or among the observances it is likely that there were some that on fixed days abstained, and on fixed days did not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Whence also he says, “Let every man be fully persuaded in his own mind.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And in this way he released those who kept the observances from fear, by saying that the thing was indifferent, and he removed also the quarrelsomeness of those who attacked them, by showing that it was no very desirable (or urgent, περισπούδαστον) task to be always making a trouble about these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yet it was not a very desirable task, not in its own nature, but on account of the time chosen, and because they were novices in the faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For when he is writing to the Colossians, it is with great earnestness that he forbids it, saying, “Beware lest any man spoil you through philosophy and vain deceit, after the traditions of men, after the elements of the world, and not after Christ.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And again, “Let no man judge you in meat or in drink” (ib.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 16), and, “let no man beguile you of your reward.” (ib.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And when writing to the Galatians with great precision, he exacts of them Christian spirit and perfectness in this matter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But here he does not use this vehemency, because the faith was lately planted in them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Let us therefore not apply the phrase, “Let every man be persuaded in his own mind,” to all subjects.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For when he is speaking of doctrines, hear what he says, “If any one preacheth unto you any gospel other than that ye have received, let him be accursed” (Gal. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 9), “even” if it be “an angel.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And again, “I fear lest by any means as the serpent beguiled Eve through his subtilty, so your minds should be corrupted.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And in writing to the Philippians, he says, “Beware of dogs, beware of evil workers, beware of the concision.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But with the Romans, since it was not yet the proper time for setting things of this sort right, “Let every man,” he says, “be fully persuaded in his own mind.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he had been speaking of fasting.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It was to clear away the vanity of the others and to release these from fear then, that he said as follows:

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “He that regardeth the day, regardeth it unto the Lord; and he that regardeth not the day, to the Lord he doth not regard it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And, “He that eateth, eateth to the Lord, for he giveth God thanks; and he that eateth not, to the Lord he eateth not, and giveth God thanks.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He still keeps to the same subject.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what he means is about this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: The thing is not concerned with fundamentals.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the thing requisite is, if this person and the other are acting for God’s sake, the thing requisite is (these words are repeated 3 mss.), if both terminate in thanksgiving.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For indeed both this man and that give thanks to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: If then both do give thanks to God, the difference is no great one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But let me draw your notice to the way in which here also he aims unawares a blow at the Judaizers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if the thing required be this, the “giving of thanks,” it is plain enough that he which eateth it is that “giveth thanks,” and not “he which eateth not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For how should he, while he still holds to the Law?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: As then he told the Galatians, “As many of you as are justified by the Law are fallen from grace” (Gal. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 4); so here he hints it only, but does not unfold it so much.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For as yet it was not time to do so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But for the present he bears with it (see p.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 337): but by what follows he gives it a further opening.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For where he says,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For none of us liveth unto himself, and no man dieth unto himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For whether we live, we live unto the Lord; and whether we die, we die unto the Lord,” by this too he makes the same clearer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For how can he that liveth unto the Law, be living unto Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But this is not the only thing that he effects by this, he also holds back the person who was in so much haste for their being set right, and persuades him to be patient, by showing that it is impossible for God to despise them, but that in due time He will set them right.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What is the force then of “none of us liveth to himself?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It means, We are not free, we have a Master who also would have us live, and willeth not that we die, and to whom both of these are of more interest than to us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For by what is here said he shows that he hath a greater concern for us than we have ourselves, and considereth more than we do, as well our life to be wealth, as our death to be a loss.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For we do not die to ourselves alone, but to our Master also, if we do die.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But by death here he means that from the faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: However, this were enough to convince us that He taketh care for us, in that it is to Him we live, and to Him we die.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Still he is not satisfied with saying this, but proceeds further.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For after saying, “Whether we live, therefore, or die, we are the Lord’s,” and passing from that death to the physical one, that he may not give an appearance of harshness to his language, he gives another very great indication of His care for us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now of what kind is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For to this end Christ both died, and rose, and revived, that He might be Lord both of the dead and living.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And so let us at least convince thee, that He is thoughtful for our salvation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For had He not had this great care for us, where were the need of the Dispensation (or Incarnation, οἰκονομίας)?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He then that hath shown so much anxiety about our becoming His, as to take the form of a servant, and to die, will He despise us after we have become so?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This cannot be so, assuredly it cannot!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Nor would He choose to waste so much pains.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For to this end (he says) he also died,” as if any one were to say, Such an one will not have the heart to despise his servant.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he minded his own purse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For indeed we are not so much in love with money, as is He with our salvation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Wherefore it was not money, but His own Blood that He gave as bail for us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And for this cause He would not have the heart to give them up, for whom He had laid down so great a price.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: See too how he shows that His power also is unspeakable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he says, “to this end He both died and revived, that He might be Lord both of the dead and the living.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And above he said, “for whether we live or die, we are His.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: See what a wide extended Mastery! see what unconquerable might! see what exact providence over us!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For tell me not, he means, of the living.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Even for the departed He taketh care.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if He doth of the departed, it is quite plain that He doth of the living also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For He hath not omitted any point for this Mastery, making out for Himself more claims than men do, and especially beside all other things in order to take care of us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For a man puts down money, and for this clings strongly to his own slave.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But He Himself paid down His death; and the salvation of one who was purchased at so great a price, and the Mastery over whom He had gained with so much anxiety and trouble, He is not likely to count of no value.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But this he says to make the Judaizer abashed, and to persuade him to call to mind the greatness of the benefit, and how that when dead he had come to be alive, and that there was nothing that he gained from the Law, and how that it would be the last degree of unfeelingness, to leave Him Who had shown so much care toward him, and run away back to the Law.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: After attacking him then sufficiently, he relaxes again, and says,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “But why dost thou judge thy brother? or why dost thou set at nought thy brother?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And so he seems to be setting them upon a level, but from that he has said, he shows that the difference between them is great.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: First then by the appellation of “brother” he does away with disputatiousness, and then also by calling that awful day to their mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For after saying, “Why dost thou set at nought thy brother?” he proceeds, “For we shall all stand before the judgment-seat of Christ.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And he seems indeed to be again rebuking the more advanced in saying this, but he is putting the mind of the Judaizer to confusion by not only calling for his reverence to the benefit that had been done him, but also making him afraid of the punishment to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For we shall all,” he says, “stand before the judgment-seat of Christ.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For it is written, As I live, saith the Lord, every knee shall bow to Me, and every tongue shall confess to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: So then every one of us shall give account of himself to God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: See how he again puts his mind into confusion, while he seems to be rebuking the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he intimates some such thing, as if he had said, How does it affect you?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Are you to be punished for him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But this he does not say, but hints at it by putting it in a milder form, and saying, “For we shall all stand before the judgment-seat of Christ:” and, “So then every one of us shall give account of himself to God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And he introduces the prophet in witness of the subjection of all to Him, yea a subjection extended even to those in the Old Testament, and of all absolutely.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he does not barely say every one shall worship, but “shall confess,” that is, shall given an account of what he has done.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Be in anxiety then as seeing the Master of all sitting on his judgment-seat, and do not make schisms and divisions in the Church, by breaking away from grace, and running over to the Law.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the Law also is His.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And why say I so of the Law?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Even those in the Law and those before the Law are His.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And it is not the Law that will demand an account of thee, but Christ, of thee and of all the human race.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: See how he has released us from the fear of the Law.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then that he may not seem to be saying this to frighten them for the occasion, but to have come to it in the course he had proposed himself, he again keeps to the same subject, and says,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Let us not therefore judge one another any more: but judge this rather, that no man put a stumbling-block or an occasion to fall in his brother’s way.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This does not apply to one less than the other: wherefore it may well fit with both, both the advanced man that was offended at the observance of meats, and the unadvanced that stumbled at the vehement rebuke given him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But consider, I pray you, the great punishment we shall suffer, if we give offence at all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if in a case where the thing was against law, yet, as they rebuked unseasonably, he forbade their doing it, in order that a brother might not be made to offend and stumble; when we give an offence without having anything to set right even, what treatment shall we deserve?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if not saving others be a crime (and that it is so, he who buried the talent proves), what will be the effect of giving him offence also?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But what if he gives himself the offence, you may say, by being weak?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Why this is just why thou oughtest to be patient.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if he were strong, then he would not require so much attention.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But now, since he is of the feebler sort, he does on this ground need considerable care.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Let us then yield him this, and in all respects bear his burdens, as it is not of our own sins only that we shall have to give an account, but for those also wherein we cause others to offend.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if that account, were even by itself hard to pass, when these be added too, how are we to be saved?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And let us not suppose, that if we can find accomplices in our sins, that will be an excuse; as this will prove an addition to our punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Since the serpent too was punished more than the woman, as was the woman likewise more than the man (1 Tim. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 14); and Jezebel also was punished more severely than Ahab, who had seized the vineyard; for it was she that devised the whole matter, and caused the king to offend.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: (1 Kings xxi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And therefore thou, when thou art the author of destruction to others, wilt suffer more severely than those who have been subverted by thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For sinning is not so ruinous as leading others also into the same.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Wherefore he speaks of those who “not only do the same, but have pleasure in them that do them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And so when we see any sinning, let us, so far from thrusting them on, even pull them back from the pit of iniquity, that we may not have to be punished for the ruin of others besides ourselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And let us be continually in mind of the awful judgment-seat, of the stream of fire, of the chains never to be loosed, of the darkness with no light, the gnashing of teeth, and the venomous worm.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Ah, but God is merciful!”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Are these then mere words? and was not that rich man punished for despising Lazarus?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Are not the foolish virgins cast out of the Bride-chamber?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Do not they who did not feed Him go away into “the fire prepared for the devil?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Will not he that hath soiled garments be “bound hand and foot” (ib. xxii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 13), and go to ruin?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Will, not he that demanded the hundred pence to be paid, be given over to the tormentors?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Is not that said of the adulterers true, that “their worm shall not die, nor their fire be quenched?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Are these but mere threats then?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yea, it is answered.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And from what source pray dost thou venture to make such an assertion, and that too when thou passest judgment of thine own opinion?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Why, I shall be able to prove the contrary, both from what He said, and from what He did.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: (See John v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if you will not believe by the punishments that are to come, at least believe by those that have happened already.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For what have happened, and have come forth into reality, surely are not threats and words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Who then was it that flooded the whole world, and affected that baleful wreck, and the utter destruction of our whole race!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Who was it that after this hurled those thunders and lightnings upon the land of Sodom?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Who that drowned all Egypt in the sea?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Who that consumed the six hundred thousand men in the wilderness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Who that burnt up the synagogue of Abiram?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Who that bade the earth open her mouth for the company of Core and Dathan, and swallow them up?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Who that carried off the threescore and ten thousand at one sweep in David’s time?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Shall I mention also those that were punished individually!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Cain, who was given up to a continual vengeance? (the son of) Charmi, who was stoned with his whole family?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Or him, that suffered the same thing for gathering sticks on the sabbath?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: The forty children who were consumed by those beasts, and obtained no pardon even on the score of their age?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And if you would see these same things even after the times of grace, just consider what great suffering the Jews had, how the women ate their children, some roasting them, and some consuming them in other ways: how after being given up to irremediable famine, and wars varied and severe, they threw all previous catastrophes into the shade by the exceeding greatness of their own calamities.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For that it was Christ Who did these things unto them, hear Him declaring as much, both by parables, and clearly and explicitly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: By parables, as when He says, “But those that would not that I should reign over them, bring hither and slay them” (Luke xix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 27); and by that of the vineyard, and that of the marriage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But clearly and explicitly, as when He threatens that they shall fall by the edge of the sword, and shall be led away captive into the nations, and there shall be upon the earth “distress of nations with perplexity, at the roaring of the sea and waves; men’s hearts failing them for fear.” (ib. xxi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “And there shall be tribulation, such as there never was, no, nor ever shall be.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: (Matt. xxiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what a punishment Ananias too and Sapphira suffered, for the theft of a few pieces of money, ye all know.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Seest thou not the daily calamities also?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Or have these too not taken place?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Seest thou not now men that are pining with famine? those that suffer elephantiasis, or are maimed in body? those that live in constant poverty, those that suffer countless irreparable evils?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now then will it be reasonable for some to be punished, and some not?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if God be not unjust (and unjust He is not), thou also wilt assuredly suffer punishment, if thou sinnest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if because He is merciful He doth not punish, then ought not these either to have been punished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But now because of these words of yours, God even here punisheth many, that when ye believe not the words of the threatening, the deeds of vengeance ye may at least believe.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And since things of old do not affright you so much, by things which happen in every generation, He correcteth those that in every generation are growing listless.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what is the reason, it may be said, why He doth not punish all here?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: That He may give the others an interval for repentance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Why then does He not take vengeance upon all in the next world?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It is lest many should disbelieve in His providence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: How many robbers are there who have been taken, and how many that have left this life unpunished?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Where is the mercy of God then? it is my turn now to ask of thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For supposing no one at all had vengeance taken upon him, then you might have taken refuge in this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But now that some are punished, and some are not, though they be the worse sinners, how can it be reasonable that there be not the same punishments for the same sins?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: How can those punished appear to be else than wronged?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What reason is there then why all are not punished here?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hear His own defence for these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For when some had died by the falling of a tower on them: He said to those who raised a question upon this, “Suppose ye that they were sinners above all men?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: I tell you nay, but except ye repent, ye shall all likewise perish” (Luke xiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 4, 5); so exhorting us not to feel confident when others suffer punishment, and we ourselves, though we have committed many transgressions, do not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For except we change our conduct, we assuredly shall suffer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And how, it may be said, is it that we are to be punished without end for sinning a short time here? how, I ask, is it that in this world, those who in a short moment of time have done one murder, are condemned to constant punishment in the mines?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “But it is not God that does this,” it may be said.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: How then came He to keep the man with a palsy for thirty and eight years in so great punishments?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For that it was for sins that He punished him, hear what He says, “Behold, thou art made whole, sin no more.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Still it is said, he found a release.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But the case is not so with the other life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For that there, there will never be any release, hear from His own mouth, “Their worm will not die, nor their fire be quenched.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And “these shall go into everlasting life, but these into everlasting punishment.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now if the life be eternal, the punishment is eternal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Seest thou not how severely He threatened the Jews?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then have the things threatened come to pass, or were those that were told them a mere talk?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “One stone shall not remain upon another.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And has it remained?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But what, when He says, “There shall be tribulation such as hath not been?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: (Matt. xxiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Has it not come then?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Read the history of Josephus, and thou wilt not be able to draw thy breath even, at only hearing what they suffered for their doings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This I say, not that I may pain you, but that I may make you secure, and lest by having humored you overmuch, I should but make a way for the endurance of sorer punishments.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For why, pray, dost thou not deem it right thou shouldest be punished for sinning?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hath He not told thee all beforehand?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hath He not threatened thee? not come to thy aid? not done things even without number for thy salvation’s sake?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Gave He thee not the laver of Regeneration, and forgave He not all thy former sins?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hath He not after this forgiveness, and the laver, also given thee the succor of repentance if thou sin?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hath He not made the way to forgiveness of sins, even after all this, easy to thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hear then what He hath enjoined: “If thou forgive thy neighbor, I also will forgive thee” (ib. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 14), He says.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What hardship is there in this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “If ye judge the cause of the fatherless, and see that the widow have right, come and let us converse together,” He saith, “and if your sins be as purple, I will make them white as snow.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What labor is there here?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Tell thy sins, that thou mayest be justified.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What hardship is there in this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Redeem thy sins with alms.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What toilsomeness is there in this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: The Publican said, “Be merciful to me a sinner,” and “went down home justified.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: (Luke xviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What labor is it to imitate the Publican?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And wilt thou not be persuaded even after this that there is punishment and vengeance?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: At that rate thou wilt deny that even the devil is punished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For, “Depart,” He says, “into the fire prepared for the devil and his angels.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now if there be no hell, then neither is he punished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if he is punished, it is plain that we shall also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For we also have disobeyed, even if it be not in the same way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And how comest thou not to be afraid to speak such daring things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For when thou sayest that God is merciful, and doth not punish, if He should punish he will be found in thy case to be no longer merciful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: See then unto what language the devil leadeth you?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what are the monks that have taken up with the mountains, and yield examples of such manifold self-denial, to go away without their crown?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if the wicked are not to be punished, and there is no recompense made to any one, some one else will say, perhaps, that neither are the good crowned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Nay, it will be said, For this is suitable with God, that there should be a kingdom only, and not a hell.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Well then, shall the whoremonger, and the adulterer, and the man who hath done evils unnumbered, enjoy the same advantages with the man who has exhibited soberness and holiness, and Paul is to stand with Nero, or rather even the devil with Paul?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if there be no hell and yet there will be a Resurrection of all, then the wicked will attain to the same good things!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And who would say this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Who even of men that were quite crazed? or rather, which of the devils even would say this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For even they confess that there is a hell.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Wherefore also they cried out and said, “Art Thou come hither to torment us before the time?” (ib. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: How then comest thou not to fear and tremble, when even the devils confess what thyself art denying?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Or how is it that thou dost not see who is the teacher of these evil doctrines?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he who deceived the first man, and under the pretext of greater hopes, threw them out even of the blessings they had in possession, he it is who now suggests the saying and fancying of these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And for this reason he persuades some to suspect there is no hell, that he may thrust them into hell.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: As God on the other hand threateneth hell, and made hell ready, that by coming to know of it thou mightest so live as not to fall into hell.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And yet if, when there is a hell, the devil persuades thee to these things, how came the devils to confess it, if it did not exist, whose aim and desire it is that we should not suspect anything of the kind, that through fearlessness we might become the more listless, and so fall with them into that fire?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: How then (it will be said) came they to confess it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It was through their not bearing the compulsion laid upon them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Taking all these things into consideration then, let those who talk in this way leave off deceiving both themselves and others since even for these words of theirs they will be punished for detracting (διασύροντες) from those awful things, and relaxing the vigor of many who are minded to be in earnest, and do not even do as much as those barbarians, for they, though they were ignorant of everything, when they heard that the city was to be destroyed, were so far from disbelieving, that they even groaned, and girded themselves with sackcloth, and were confounded, and did not cease to use every means until they had allayed the wrath.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But dost thou, who hast had so great experience of facts and of teaching, make light of what is told thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: The contrary then will be thy fate.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For as they through fear of the words had not to undergo the vengeance in act, so thou who despisest the threatening by words, wilt have to undergo the punishment in very deed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And if now what thou art told seems a fable to thee, it will not, however, seem so when the very things convince thee, in that Day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Have you never noticed what He did even in this world?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: How when He met with two thieves, He counted them not worthy of the same estate, but one He led into the Kingdom, and the other He sent away into Hell?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And why speak I of a robber and murderer?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For even the Apostle He did not spare, when he had become a traitor, but even when He saw him rushing to the halter, and hanging, and bursting asunder in the midst (for he did “burst asunder, and all his bowels gushed out”) (Acts i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 18), still when He foresaw all these things, He let him suffer all the same, giving thee from the present a proof of all that is in the other world also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Do not then cheat yourselves, through being persuaded of the devil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: These devices are his.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if both judges, and masters, and teachers, and savages, respect the good, and punish the evil, with what reason is the contrary to be the case with God, while the good man and he who is not so are deemed worthy of the same estate?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And when will they leave off their wickedness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For they who now are expecting punishment, and are amongst so many terrors, those from the judges and from the laws, and yet do not for this depart from iniquity; when on their departing this life they are to lay aside even this fear, and are not only not to be cast into hell, but are even to obtain a kingdom; when will they leave doing wickedly?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Is this then mercy, pray? to add to wickedness, to set up rewards for iniquity, to count the sober and the unchastened, the faithful and the irreligious, Paul and the devil, to have the same deserts?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But how long am I to be trifling?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Wherefore I exhort you to get you free from this madness, and having grown to be your own masters, persuade your souls to fear and to tremble, that they may at once be saved from the hell to come, and may, after passing the life in this world soberly, attain unto the good things to come by the grace and love towards man, etc.
